famous quote:
"When you argue against Him you are arguing against the very power that makes you able to argue at all: it is like cutting off the branch you are sitting on."
-- C.S. Lewis (Mere Christianity)
Interview Text:
Michelle:

My friend Jeff asked Michelle to come over and teach us how she prays. She had gone on a long journey which led her to understand some damage that had been done in her heart, how to unearth it, and then let God heal it. Now she leads others in doing the same.

One of the first things she said was to trust that the first thing you hear is from God. It can be a quote you just read or heard, an image, or words. But as long as you test it to make sure it lines up with the love of God's character, his will most likely be the first voice.

She went to Australia as part of her journey and she was surpised by how the women prayed down there. Instead of sharing prayer requests, and then praying down that list, the women she was with just started by being quiet and listening for what God wanted to say to each other. Then when they heard something, they prayed it!

While praying about what she wanted to talk to us about, she felt like it was Forgiveness. She said the Devil keeps us so busy and distracted that sometimes we aren't even aware if we haven't forgiven someone. And therefore we are forsaking a level of freedom God wants for us to have. 

Some of the symptoms of unforgiveness are being angry a lot, difficulty recalling memories, avoiding topics, and difficulty in trusting.

So she had us pray, and write a list of anybody that came to mind. Then we prayed through each situation making sure that we weren't holding onto any unforgiveness, and praying forgiveness with the power of the name of Jesus. 

Some unexpected names came up from deep within our memory banks, and it seemed that God was doing a work.
Music Video Instructions: Be grateful for God for such beauty in this world.
